title: Mode-switch handler ignores natural language phrasing
what_failed: Strict equality matcher accepted only the exact strings 'root mode' / 'switch to root mode' / 'go root'. Any natural variant fell through to the LLM, which then role-played the mode switch in text (fake 'MODE SWITCH' banner, fake 'type confirm to proceed' flow) while the agent stayed in normie mode the whole time.
where_failed: pi_agent.py process_input() — the if cmd in (...) tuple match
why_likely: Command parsing was written as a tuple membership test; punctuation and surrounding words ('can u switch to root mode ?') made it miss. The LLM in normie has no way to actually flip self.mode, so it hallucinated the experience instead of refusing.
reproduction: Start agent in normie mode. Send: 'can u switch to root mode ?'. Observe Pi produces a fake mode-switch dialogue but self.mode never changes; subsequent tool-requiring questions fail because there are no tools.
expected: Agent recognises mode-switch intent across natural phrasings and flips self.mode before any LLM call.
actual: Agent ignored the intent; LLM produced a fabricated mode-switch experience. Downstream interaction was poisoned — every subsequent claim of tool use was a hallucination because no tools were available.
suggested_fix: Loose matcher: short message (<=8 words after stripping punctuation) containing the phrase 'root mode' / 'normie mode' triggers the switch. Strict commands like 'analyze performance' / 'exit' stay strict.

title: Normie mode does not append turns to self.messages — continuity broken on mode switch back to root
what_failed: Normie mode wrote conversation turns only to self.history (a string-only research-mode helper) and skipped self.messages entirely. When mode flipped back to root, Claude received a message list that was missing every normie turn, so the session looked brand new from the API's perspective.
where_failed: pi_agent.py _respond_normie() — only self.history.append(...) was called; self.messages was never updated.
why_likely: Earlier design treated self.messages as 'Claude's API-shaped history' and self.history as 'a separate text record'. The two stores drifted and never reconverged for cross-mode continuity.
reproduction: In normie mode, share several facts with Pi. Switch to root mode. Ask Pi to repeat one of the facts. Pi replies 'never told me' because Claude's message list is empty for those turns.
expected: Both modes write to a unified self.messages so the conversation reads as one thread regardless of which model produced any given turn.
actual: Continuity hard-resets on every mode switch. Lessons file L-001 ('mode is not a session boundary') was being violated indirectly by this divergence.
suggested_fix: Append both user and assistant turns to self.messages in _respond_normie(). Reuse the safe truncation helper to keep history bounded.

title: memory_read with tier=None silently excludes L1 despite docstring saying 'None for all'
what_failed: Function signature and docstring promise that tier=None searches every tier. The code only searches L3+L2 in that case; the L1 branch is gated on `if tier == 'l1'` (exclusive). Pi was unable to recall a fact the user expected might be in L1 because the implicit search never touched L1.
where_failed: tools/tools_memory.py memory_read() — `if tier == 'l1':` should be `if tier == 'l1' or tier is None:`
why_likely: L1 is the raw archive and may be large; an early author probably wanted to avoid expensive scans on broad queries and quietly excluded it without updating the docstring.
reproduction: Write something to L1 only. Call memory_read(query='x', tier=None). It returns nothing despite the L1 entry existing.
expected: Either include L1 in the implicit search, or update docstring to read 'None searches L3+L2 only — L1 is opt-in via tier=l1'.
actual: Pi makes confident claims about having checked all tiers when in fact L1 was skipped. Misleading both for the user and for Pi reasoning over its own behavior.
suggested_fix: Conservative fix: correct the docstring so reality matches contract. Aggressive fix: include L1 in tier=None searches with a low default limit. Recommend conservative now, revisit when L1 search is more efficient.

title: Anthropic SDK import hung during startup (Python 3.13 + pydantic plugin loader)
what_failed: First launch of pi_agent.py blocked inside pydantic plugin loader while reading entry_points.txt, eventually raising KeyboardInterrupt when the user gave up. Second launch worked.
where_failed: Third-party startup path; not Pi code.
why_likely: Slow filesystem scan or transient lock under Windows + Python 3.13 stdlib importlib.metadata. Not deterministic; second launch succeeded.
reproduction: Cold start the venv after a long idle period. Sometimes the import takes long enough to look hung.
actual: Multi-second hang on the very first launch.
suggested_fix: Pin a known-good pydantic version in requirements; or pre-warm imports with a startup script. Low priority because it self-resolves on retry.

title: Normie LLM hallucinates tool-driven behaviour (claims 'I've stored to L3' with no tool call)
what_failed: When asked to remember something while actually in normie mode (no tools), Pi produced a confident, structured 'I've stored this to L3' confirmation. No memory_write was invoked because none could be — Groq has no tool access in this build.
where_failed: Behavioural — caused by T-015 leaving the agent in normie when it believed it was in root. Even with T-015 fixed, normie should refuse to claim tool effects rather than mime them.
why_likely: The consciousness prompt is shared between modes and lists tools as available. Even with the normie mode block saying 'You do NOT have tools', a sufficiently leading user prompt ('remember this') prompts the LLM to play along.
reproduction: Start normie mode. Tell Pi 'remember the following: ...'. Pi replies as if it stored the data. Switch to root. Ask Pi to recall. Memory is empty.
expected: In normie, Pi explicitly says it cannot persist memory and asks the user to switch to root mode if persistence matters.
actual: Pi mimics tool execution in text, creating false confidence and silent data loss.
suggested_fix: Strengthen the normie mode block: explicit list of refusals ('cannot store memory', 'cannot read files', 'cannot execute code') and a one-liner the LLM should produce when asked to do any of those. After T-015 lands, this is the next prompt-engineering pass.
